titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/257

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=35 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:01:35.158013+00 | translation moa=36 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:01:37.162891+00

div.p:
Alex’ dei Gratia Rex Scott’ Omnibus probis Hominibus totius terre sue Clericis et Laicis Salutem
Sciant presentes et futuri nos concessisse et Hac Carta nostra confirmasse finalem concordiam . factam
inter Abbatem et conuentum Sancte crucis de Edneburg’ ex una parte et Inger’ de Baill’ ex alia .
super diuisis terraRum ecclesiaRum eiusdem abbatis et Conuentus scilicet de Kircost’ et de Kirkebrid’
tenend’ et Habend’ dictis Abbati et Conuentui de predicto Inger’ et heredibus suis . per rectas di
uisas contentas in carta ipsius Inger’ et cum omnibus rectitudinibus et pertinentiis suis . ita
Libere et Quiete . plenarie et honorifice sicut Carta eiusdem Inger’ de Baill’ dictis Abbati
et conuentui confecta plennius iuste testatur . Saluo seruitio nostro . Testibus . Magistro Math’ cancellario .
Willelmo Cum’ Comite de Buch’ . Iust’ Scott’ . Inger’ de Baill’ . Iohanne de haya . Walt’ Cum’ . dauid Cum’
Willelmo de Munfichet’ . Alexandro de Seton’ . Ph’ de Maleuill’ vicecomite de Aberden’ . dauid de Lindes’ .
Apud Aberden’ Octauo die Iulij . Anno Regni nostri Quartodecimo

Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know that we have granted and by this our charter made firm the final agreement made between the aboot and convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh on the one side and Ingram de Balliol on the other, over the marches of the lands of the churches of the same abbot and convent, that is, at Kirkconstantine and at Kirkbride, for the said abbot and convent to hold and to have of the aforesaid Ingram and his heirs by the correct bounds described in Ingram’s own charter and with all their revenues and pertinents, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably as the charter of the same Ingram de Balliol drawn up for the said abbot and convent the more fully declares. Saving our service. As witnesses, Master Matthew, chancellor, William Comyn, earl of Buchan, justiciar of Scotland, Ingram de Balliol, John de la Haye, Walter Comyn, David Comyn, William de Munfichet [Muschet], Alexander of Seton, Philip de Malville, sheriff of Aberdeen, David of Lindsay. At Aberdeen on the eighth day of July, in the fourteenth year of our reign.
